Crypto Loan Calculator

Free Crypto Loan Calculator. Estimate crypto-backed loan amount, interest costs, margin call threshold, and liquidation risk for DeFi and CeFi loans.

By Konstantin Iakovlev · Updated 2026-05-12
Auto-calculates as you type. Keep LTV conservative and monitor margin-call/liquidation thresholds before borrowing.

Crypto-backed loans carry risk of liquidation if collateral value drops. Rates and terms vary by platform. Not financial advice.
Quick answer: Crypto-backed loan interest = Principal × APR × (Term/12). Borrowing $20,000 against BTC collateral at 8% APR for 12 months costs $1,600 in interest. Compare LTV ratios across platforms.

How to use Crypto Loan Calculator

The Crypto Loan Calculator computes the interest cost, liquidation price, and LTV (loan-to-value) dynamics for a cryptocurrency-backed loan. Enter your collateral asset and amount, the loan amount in USD or stablecoin, the annual interest rate, and your loan duration to see total interest owed, effective APR, and the collateral price level that would trigger liquidation.

Use it before taking a crypto loan on platforms like Nexo, BlockFi, or Compound to understand the true cost and risk of leverage. The liquidation price calculation is especially valuable — it shows how much your collateral can drop before the lender liquidates your position to cover the loan, helping you maintain a safe margin of safety.

Input guide and assumptions

Collateral amount is the crypto you're pledging as security (e.g., 1 BTC). Collateral value is auto-filled at current price. Loan amount in USD is how much you're borrowing. LTV ratio (loan amount / collateral value) typically must stay below 50–80% depending on the platform — the calculator shows your current LTV and the critical liquidation LTV threshold.

Annual interest rate varies by platform and collateral type — ranges from 6–18% for major assets on CeFi platforms to 2–5% on DeFi protocols. Loan duration determines total interest. The maintenance LTV field shows at what LTV ratio the lender will liquidate — typically 10–15% above your initial LTV to provide a buffer for market volatility.

How to interpret results correctly

Crypto loan calculations model the complete cost structure of borrowing against crypto collateral — including interest rates, collateral requirements (loan-to-value ratio), liquidation thresholds, and the opportunity cost of locked collateral. A typical CeFi crypto loan at 8% APR with 50% LTV on $100,000 BTC collateral produces $50,000 in borrowable funds at $4,000 annual interest cost, while the locked BTC cannot be staked or used in DeFi for additional yield.

The critical risk metric in crypto-backed lending is the distance to liquidation — the percentage price decline in your collateral that triggers forced selling. With a 50% LTV loan and 80% liquidation threshold, your collateral can decline 37.5% before liquidation. In crypto markets where 30-50% corrections are common, this margin is dangerously thin. Always model your liquidation price and set alerts at 20% and 10% above that threshold.

Practical scenarios and planning workflow

An investor has 10 BTC worth $650,000 and needs $200,000 for a real estate down payment. A crypto loan at 8% APR with 50% LTV requires posting 6.15 BTC as collateral ($400,000 at current prices) to borrow $200,000. Annual interest: $16,000. Liquidation occurs if BTC drops below $39,000 (40% decline). The investor must decide if keeping BTC exposure justifies $16,000 annual cost versus simply selling 3.08 BTC.

A DeFi borrower uses Aave to borrow $50,000 USDC against $100,000 in ETH at 3.5% variable APR. Liquidation threshold: 82.5% LTV. At current prices, ETH can decline 17.5% before liquidation. With ETH's historical 30-day volatility of 25%, the probability of a 17.5% decline in any given month is approximately 20-25% — making this position statistically likely to face liquidation pressure within 4-5 months.

Risk and execution checklist

  1. Before taking a crypto loan, calculate: (1) the full interest cost over the expected loan duration, (2) the liquidation price of your collateral, (3) the historical probability of a decline reaching that liquidation price, and (4) whether selling a portion of collateral would be more cost-effective than paying loan interest.
  2. Monitor your loan health factor daily during volatile markets. Set alerts at 20% above your liquidation threshold to provide adequate response time for adding collateral or partial repayment.

Common mistakes to avoid

  • Underestimating the probability of liquidation. Crypto markets frequently experience 30-50% corrections. If your liquidation price is 35% below current price, historical data shows this distance is breached in the majority of 12-month periods. Factor in realistic drawdown probabilities.
  • Taking out crypto loans to buy more crypto (recursive leverage). This amplifies both gains and losses and creates cascading liquidation risk. If the collateral asset declines, your loan becomes undercollateralized while the purchased asset also declines — a double loss that can wipe out the position entirely.

Performance benchmarks and expectation ranges

CeFi lending rate benchmarks: major platforms offer 6-12% APR for crypto-backed USD loans. DeFi variable rates fluctuate more widely: 2-5% during low demand, 15-30% during high demand. Comparing current rates against these benchmarks reveals whether current borrowing conditions are favorable or expensive.

Liquidation frequency benchmarks: during the May 2022 crash, over $1 billion in crypto loans were liquidated in a single week. Analysis shows that loans with LTV above 65% at the time of the crash had liquidation rates exceeding 40%. Maintaining LTV below 50% historically reduces liquidation probability to under 10% even during major corrections.

Data hygiene and model maintenance

Review all active loan positions weekly: check current LTV, distance to liquidation in both percentage and dollar terms, and accumulated interest charges. Update your liquidation price alerts after any collateral price movement exceeding 10%.

Maintain a loan cost ledger tracking total interest paid per loan over its lifetime. Comparing total interest cost against the benefit of maintaining the loan (avoiding capital gains tax, maintaining crypto exposure) ensures the borrowing remains economically rational.

Frequently asked questions

How does a DeFi crypto loan work?

You deposit crypto (usually ETH, BTC, or stablecoins) as collateral on Aave or Compound, then borrow up to a percentage of its value (typically 50–80% LTV). Interest accrues per block, no credit check needed. If your collateral price drops and LTV exceeds the liquidation threshold, the protocol auto-sells your collateral.

What is LTV and why does it matter?

Loan-to-Value is debt divided by collateral value. Borrow $5,000 against $10,000 ETH = 50% LTV. Aave's ETH liquidation threshold is 82.5%, so if ETH drops 40% your LTV jumps to 83% and 50% of your position gets liquidated with a 5% penalty. Most cautious borrowers stay under 40% LTV for safety.

What are typical crypto loan interest rates?

Stablecoin borrowing rates on Aave V3 ranged 4–12% APY in 2025, ETH 1–3%, BTC 0.5–2%. CeFi platforms like Nexo offer 0–13.9% depending on loyalty tier. Variable rates spike during high demand — USDC borrowing hit 25% on Aave during the May 2024 leverage cycle.

What price drop will liquidate my loan?

If you borrow at 50% LTV with an 80% liquidation threshold, your collateral can fall 37.5% before liquidation. At 70% LTV, only 12.5% drop triggers it. The formula: max drop = 1 − (current LTV ÷ liquidation threshold). Always model a 50% crash before borrowing — ETH has done that 5 times since 2018.

What's the biggest mistake DeFi borrowers make?

Borrowing at 70%+ LTV thinking they can "manage it actively." Liquidations happen in minutes during flash crashes — the May 2021 crash liquidated $2.5B of crypto loans in 24 hours. Cap initial LTV at 30–40%, set price alerts 20% above liquidation, and keep stablecoins ready to repay or top up collateral.

Aave vs Compound vs CeFi — which is best?

Aave V3 has the deepest liquidity ($12B+ TVL) and broadest asset support including E-Mode for correlated-asset higher LTV. Compound is simpler but smaller. CeFi (Nexo, Ledn) offers fixed rates and fiat withdrawal but adds counterparty risk — Celsius and BlockFi froze withdrawals in 2022. DeFi is non-custodial but requires gas (~$5–50 on mainnet).
